violations.text: The side double doors to the outside, located in the dining area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was under 150 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be (150-400) ppm. PIC instructed to only use sanitizer from back three bay sink and not front one. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The ice cream prep unit was dripping/leaking inside of prep unit leading to a water build up. Ambient temperature slowly dropped to 43 degrees. Unit to be serviced. PIC stated that they would turn off unit at the end of the day to defrost unit if unit is iced over. Establishment to store TCS foods in other refrigeration units. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/19/2023

Various foods were observed held at 42-48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food recently came out of temperature. Food was moved back to walk-in to rapidly cool. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

The ice cream prep unit and the prep unit next to the hot hold unit were observed not holding adequate ambient temperature. The ice cream prep unit was observed with pooling water. Both units must be serviced/repaired/replaced prior to storing any TCS foods. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023
